featureName: BUG-058-003: flaky badge-matrix e2e test — MV3 service-worker chrome.alarms spin-up race in the triggerDrain test helper
reporter: Orchestrator directive — a genuine flake discovered while re-verifying spec 058's ./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext discharge claim (10 passed / 1 failed; failing test sideload_smoke.spec.ts:49 'badge matrix'; ~1/3 cold-run fail rate; retries:0 fails the whole suite).

executionHistory.summary: Independently re-confirmed the reported flake. `--repeat-each=15` in ONE warm process passed 15/15 (no repro); 12 SEPARATE cold `./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext -- sideload_smoke.spec.ts --grep "badge matrix"` invocations failed 2/12 (runs 4, 7) with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'create')` at fixtures.ts:192 — proving a COLD-process-start race, not a warm-fixture one. Read fixtures.ts triggerDrain (unguarded chrome.alarms.create in sw.evaluate), manifest.json (declares the `alarms` permission), and src/background/index.ts:273-274 (top-level chrome.alarms.create + onAlarm listener) and CONFIRMED the product code is correct — the bug is a test-harness MV3-SW spin-up binding race where permission-gated chrome.alarms is transiently undefined while base chrome exists.

Single bugfix-fastlane scope: harden triggerDrain() to wait INSIDE the sw.evaluate worker context for `typeof chrome?.alarms?.create === 'function'` before calling it (no cross-process TOCTOU gap), bounded to 5s/50ms and rejecting loudly on timeout. Deliberate no-shortcut decisions: NOT adding Playwright retries (forbidden flake-masking); NOT weakening expect(badge).toBe('SETUP'); NOT touching product code; in-evaluate poll over the heavier reloadServiceWorker() since we only need to wake+drain a fresh worker. SCN-058-003-01 authored.

Edited extensions/chrome-bridge/test/e2e/fixtures.ts triggerDrain() only: replaced the unguarded `chrome.alarms.create(...)` with an in-evaluate bounded readiness poll (5s deadline, 50ms interval) that waits for `typeof chrome?.alarms?.create === 'function'`, then creates the drain alarm and settles 50ms; rejects with a clear 'MV3 service-worker lifecycle race' error on timeout. Added a doc-comment explaining the MV3 SW lifecycle race. No other file changed; get_errors on fixtures.ts → no errors.

AFTER fix: 20 SEPARATE cold `./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext -- sideload_smoke.spec.ts --grep "badge matrix"` invocations → 20/20 PASS (AFTER_FIX_TALLY: pass=20 fail=0 of 20), exceeding the >=10 bar. Full `./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext` suite → 11/11 PASS (test 11 = the previously-flaky badge matrix). All runs through the sanctioned CLI surface. Evidence in report.md '### After Fix (GREEN)' + '### Test Evidence'.

Adversarial RED→GREEN documented: the 'test' is the e2e test; the adversarial methodology is the separate-cold-invocation battery (the only methodology that surfaces the flake — warm --repeat-each does not). RED = 2/12 fail before the guard; GREEN = 20/20 + 11/11 after. Reverting the in-evaluate readiness poll restores the BEFORE red. Recorded in report.md '### Regression Evidence'.

The fix is the minimal surgical change: a single in-evaluate poll in the one failing helper. Rejected heavier alternatives (full reloadServiceWorker() before drain; Playwright-side two-step readiness check that reopens the TOCTOU window). No new abstraction or helper introduced.

Determinism: the readiness check and the chrome.alarms.create call run in the SAME worker evaluate, so the binding cannot disappear between check and use; the wait is bounded (5s) and rejects loudly so a genuinely-broken worker fails clearly rather than hanging or silently passing. playwright.config.ts stays workers:1/fullyParallel:false/retries:0. 20/20 cold + 11/11 full-suite confirm stability.

No secrets touched; no request interception introduced; the assertion expect(badge).toBe('SETUP') is NOT weakened; no product/security surface (manifest permissions, CSP, alarms wiring) changed. The change is confined to a test helper's robustness. git status shows only test/e2e/fixtures.ts modified.

The canonical proof runs through the repo CLI surface (./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext): 20/20 isolated cold + 11/11 full suite. artifact-lint + state-transition-guard run at close-out against this packet (output appended to report.md '### Validation Evidence'). Parent spec 058 planning truth NOT touched.

Change-boundary containment: `git status --porcelain` shows only extensions/chrome-bridge/test/e2e/fixtures.ts modified (the untracked .vscode/ is pre-existing unrelated IDE state, not staged, not part of this fix). Product code (src/background/index.ts, manifest.json) confirmed unchanged. No config/generated and no .github/bubbles framework files touched. Parent spec 058 stays blocked; its blockingDependencies are NOT modified by this packet.

The fix carries an inline doc-comment explaining the MV3 SW lifecycle race and the no-retry-masking decision. The packet (bug.md/spec.md/design.md/scopes.md/report.md/uservalidation.md/scenario-manifest.json) records the root cause, the cold-vs-warm proof, the RED→GREEN evidence, the product-code-is-correct confirmation, the honest parent-058-stays-blocked caveat, and the Gate G088 commit-ordering note for the committing orchestrator.
G088 two-phase re-stamp: certifiedAt set after planning-truth commit bed146f9

scopeProgress.title: Make triggerDrain() robust to the MV3 SW spin-up chrome.alarms race

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane MV3 test-harness flake fix; single helper change; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: The MV3 e2e harness helper triggerDrain() (fixtures.ts:191) called chrome.alarms.create(...) with no readiness guard; on a cold MV3 service-worker spin-up the permission-gated chrome.alarms binding is transiently undefined while the base chrome namespace exists, so the call threw 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'create')' and — with playwright.config.ts retries:0 — flaked the whole ./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext suite ~1-in-6 to ~1-in-3 cold runs (reproduced 2/12).
resolvedFindings.resolution: Hardened triggerDrain() with an in-evaluate bounded readiness poll: inside the sw.evaluate worker context, wait up to 5s (50ms interval) for `typeof chrome?.alarms?.create === 'function'` before calling chrome.alarms.create(DRAIN_ALARM); reject loudly on timeout. No Playwright retries added; the assertion expect(badge).toBe('SETUP') and all product code are unchanged.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md '### Before Fix (RED)' (2/12 cold fail), '### After Fix (GREEN)' (20/20 cold + 11/11 full suite), '### Code Diff Evidence', '### Regression Evidence', '### Audit Evidence'; extensions/chrome-bridge/test/e2e/fixtures.ts triggerDrain().
